mn55:1.2: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in Jīvaka Komārabhacca’s mango grove.
mn55:11.4: This, *bhante*, is directly seen by me in the *Bhagavā*;
mn55:11.6: “The greed, Jīvaka, the hate, and the delusion because of which there would be harmfulness, discontent, or resistance — that greed, that hate, that delusion have been abandoned by the Realized One, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, made nonexistent, not liable to arise in the future.
mn55:11.7: If, Jīvaka, you said that with this in mind, I allow it.”
mn55:11.8: “Indeed, *bhante*, that is exactly what I said with this in mind.”
mn55:12.1: “Whoever, Jīvaka, slaughters a living being for the sake of the Realized One or a disciple of the Realized One produces much demerit in five ways.
mn55:12.2: When he says, ‘Go and bring such-and-such a living being,’
mn55:12.3: by this first way he produces much demerit.
mn55:12.4: When that living being, being led along with a rope around its neck, experiences *dukkha* and distress, by this second way he produces much demerit.
mn55:12.6: ‘Go and slaughter this living being,’ by this third way he produces much demerit.
mn55:12.7: When that living being, being slaughtered, experiences *dukkha* and distress, by this fourth way he produces much demerit.
mn55:12.8: When he presents the Realized One or a disciple of the Realized One with what is not allowable, by this fifth way he produces much demerit.
mn55:12.9: Whoever, Jīvaka, slaughters a living being for the sake of the Realized One or a disciple of the Realized One produces much demerit in these five ways.”
mn55:13.1: When this was said, Jīvaka Komārabhacca said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn55:13.6: Let the *Bhagavā* remember me as a lay follower who from today has gone for refuge for life.”
mn55:2.1: Then Jīvaka Komārabhacca went to the *Bhagavā*. Having gone to the *Bhagavā*, he paid homage to him and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Jīvaka Komārabhacca said this to the *Bhagavā*:
mn55:3.2: ‘They slaughter a living being for the sake of the ascetic Gotama, and the ascetic Gotama knowingly consumes meat specifically prepared for him because of that deed.’
mn55:3.3: Those who say this, *bhante* — ‘They slaughter a living being for the sake of the ascetic Gotama, and the ascetic Gotama knowingly consumes meat specifically prepared for him because of that deed’ — do they speak in accordance with what has been said by the *Bhagavā*, and not misrepresent the *Bhagavā* with what is false, and explain *dhamma* in accordance with *dhamma*, so that no legitimate consequence of their statement would give grounds for criticism?”
mn55:4.1: “Those who say this, Jīvaka — ‘They slaughter a living being for the sake of the ascetic Gotama, and the ascetic Gotama knowingly consumes meat specifically prepared for him because of that deed’ — do not speak in accordance with what I have said, and they misrepresent me with what is false and untrue.
mn55:5.1: Jīvaka, I say that meat is not to be consumed in three cases.
mn55:5.3: in these three cases, Jīvaka, I say that meat is not to be consumed.
mn55:5.4: Jīvaka, I say that meat is to be consumed in three cases.
mn55:5.5: Not seen, not heard, not suspected—
mn55:5.6: in these three cases, Jīvaka, I say that meat is to be consumed.
mn55:6.1: Here, Jīvaka, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain village or town.
mn55:6.10: it does not occur to him like that either.
mn55:6.11: He consumes that almsfood untied, uninfatuated, not swallowed up, seeing the drawback, with discernment of escape [*nissaraṇapañño*].
mn55:6.13: does that *bhikkhu* at that time intend his own harm, or another’s harm, or the harm of both?”
mn55:6.15: “Then, Jīvaka, at that time does that *bhikkhu* not take only blameless food?”
mn55:6.2: He dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettāsahagatena*], likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind imbued with goodwill [*mettāsahagatena*], vast, exalted, measureless, free from enmity, free from ill will.
mn55:6.3: A householder or a householder’s son approaches him and invites him for the next day’s meal.
mn55:6.4: If he wishes, Jīvaka, the *bhikkhu* accepts.
mn55:6.5: Then, when that night has passed, in the morning he robes himself and, taking bowl and robe, goes to the home of that householder or householder’s son. Having gone there, he sits down on the seat made ready.
mn55:6.6: That householder or householder’s son serves him with fine almsfood.
mn55:6.7: It does not occur to him:
mn55:6.8: ‘It would be good if this householder or householder’s son were to serve me with fine almsfood.’
mn55:6.9: ‘Oh, if only this householder or householder’s son would serve me in the future too with such fine almsfood’—
mn55:7.4: This, *bhante*, is directly seen by me in the *Bhagavā*;
mn55:7.6: “The greed, Jīvaka, the hate, and the delusion because of which there would be ill will — that greed, that hate, that delusion have been abandoned by the Realized One, cut off at the root, made like a palm stump, made nonexistent, not liable to arise in the future.
mn55:7.7: If, Jīvaka, you said that with this in mind, I allow it.”
mn55:7.8: “Indeed, *bhante*, that is exactly what I said with this in mind.”
mn55:8-10.1: “Here, Jīvaka, a *bhikkhu* dwells relying on a certain village or town.
mn55:8-10.10: It does not occur to him:
mn55:8-10.11: ‘It would be good if this householder or householder’s son were to serve me with fine almsfood.’
mn55:8-10.12: ‘Oh, if only this householder or householder’s son would serve me in the future too with such fine almsfood’—
mn55:8-10.13: it does not occur to him like that either.
mn55:8-10.14: He consumes that almsfood untied, uninfatuated, not swallowed up, seeing the drawback, with discernment of escape [*nissaraṇapañño*].
mn55:8-10.16: does that *bhikkhu* at that time intend his own harm, or another’s harm, or the harm of both?”
mn55:8-10.18: “Then, Jīvaka, at that time does that *bhikkhu* not take only blameless food?”
mn55:8-10.2: He dwells pervading one direction with a mind imbued with compassion [*karuṇāsahagatena*] …
mn55:8-10.3: with a mind imbued with gladness [*muditāsahagatena*] …
mn55:8-10.4: with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhāsahagatena*], he dwells pervading one direction, likewise the second, likewise the third, likewise the fourth.
mn55:8-10.5: Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every respect, he dwells pervading the whole world with a mind imbued with equanimity [*upekkhāsahagatena*], vast, exalted, measureless, free from enmity, free from ill will.
mn55:8-10.6: A householder or a householder’s son approaches him and invites him for the next day’s meal.
mn55:8-10.7: If he wishes, Jīvaka, the *bhikkhu* accepts.
mn55:8-10.8: Then, when that night has passed, in the morning he robes himself and, taking bowl and robe, goes to the home of that householder or householder’s son. Having gone there, he sits down on the seat made ready.
mn55:8-10.9: That householder or householder’s son serves him with fine almsfood.
